prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='ONLY_CWT_rf', seasonality_champion='DWT_rf', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: The validation folds show strong upward trends across all periods with ONLY_CWT_rf leading trend tracking (0.0558 error) and DWT_rf capturing seasonality best (0.8763 correlation). Model disagreement is extremely high (rmse_spread_ratio=0.86) and seasonality variance is substantial (0.68), indicating models capture patterns very differently. With early horizon specialists (CWT_catboost, ONLY_CWT_svr) differing from late horizon specialists (svr, catboost), DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model strengths while mitigating disagreement through dynamic weighting.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "ONLY_CWT_rf",
  "seasonality_champion": "DWT_rf",
  "overall_champion": "DWT_catboost",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "CWT_catboost", "late": "svr"},
  "tier1_models": ["DWT_catboost", "CWT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "FT_rf", "CWT_rf", "DWT_rf", "catboost", "ONLY_DWT_svr"],
  "tier2_models": ["ONLY_CWT_svr", "rf", "svr", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "CWT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "DWT_svr", "FT_catboost"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.8603,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "up", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "The validation folds show strong upward trends across all periods with ONLY_CWT_rf leading trend tracking (0.0558 error) and DWT_rf capturing seasonality best (0.8763 correlation). Model disagreement is extremely high (rmse_spread_ratio=0.86) and seasonality variance is substantial (0.68), indicating models capture patterns very differently. With early horizon specialists (CWT_catboost, ONLY_CWT_svr) differing from late horizon specialists (svr, catboost), DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model strengths while mitigating disagreement through dynamic weighting."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current candidate set has good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k5), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35), 1 baseline (dba_combination), 1 stacking (ridge_stacking_l250_topk5). No type has ≥4 candidates so no pruning needed. All candidates use safe anti-leakage methods (selection uses past windows, baseline never fits, weighted uses expanding rolling). Universe leaderboard shows topk_mean_per_horizon_k4 performs better but adding it would create redundancy with existing topk_mean variant. Current set is balanced and diverse - no changes required.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}
raw.statistician: {"add_names": [], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "Current candidate set is well-diversified with selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k5), weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35), stacking (ridge_stacking_l250_topk5), and DBA methods. n_windows=3 suggests following recommended_knobs for stronger regularization. relative_spread_mean=0.0814 (<0.25) indicates disagreement is not high enough to strongly favor additional robust methods. Universe leaderboards show topk_mean_per_horizon_k4 (RMSE=27117) beats current best ridge_stacking_l250_topk5 (RMSE=27775), but current set already has topk_mean_per_horizon_k5 which is similar. No changes needed - candidate set is robust and diverse.", "changes": [], "when_good": "Candidate set contains multiple method types (selection, weighted, stacking, DBA) with n_windows≤4 and moderate disagreement."}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: The validation folds show strong upward trends across all periods with ONLY_CWT_rf leading trend tracking (0.0558 error) and DWT_rf capturing seasonality best (0.8763 correlation). Model disagreement is extremely high (rmse_spread_ratio=0.86) and seasonality variance is substantial (0.68), indicating models capture patterns very differently. With early horizon specialists (CWT_catboost, ONLY_CWT_svr) differing from late horizon specialists (svr, catboost), DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model strengths while mitigating disagreement through dynamic weighting.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=11 indicates strong per-horizon specialization requiring selection methods. Pattern_analyst_insights recommends dba_combination for high disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.86). RMSE spread between best (CWT_catboost: 31208) and worst (ONLY_FT_rf: 57810) is large (~84%). topk_mean_per_horizon_k5 captures early horizon specialists while inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35 provides shrinkage stability. ridge_stacking_l250_topk5 adds meta-learning for heterogeneous horizons.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k5), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35), 1 baseline (dba_combination), 1 stacking (ridge_stacking_l250_topk5). No type has ≥4 candidates so no pruning needed. All candidates use safe anti-leakage methods (selection uses past windows, baseline never fits, weighted uses expanding rolling). Universe leaderboard shows topk_mean_per_horizon_k4 performs better but adding it would create redundancy with existing topk_mean variant. Current set is balanced and diverse - no changes required.

statistician.rationale: Current candidate set is well-diversified with selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k5), weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35), stacking (ridge_stacking_l250_topk5), and DBA methods. n_windows=3 suggests following recommended_knobs for stronger regularization. relative_spread_mean=0.0814 (<0.25) indicates disagreement is not high enough to strongly favor additional robust methods. Universe leaderboards show topk_mean_per_horizon_k4 (RMSE=27117) beats current best ridge_stacking_l250_topk5 (RMSE=27775), but current set already has topk_mean_per_horizon_k5 which is similar. No changes needed - candidate set is robust and diverse.
statistician.when_good: Candidate set contains multiple method types (selection, weighted, stacking, DBA) with n_windows≤4 and moderate disagreement.
